zh.Amesbury, Wiltshire, England: 英國維爾爾阿姆斯波利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh Le Moyne於大約1148年出生在英國維爾爾阿姆斯波利時，他的父親Adam Le Moyne32歲，母親Baroness NN Le Moyne Hamelyn28歲。他於1172年與Estcotte在英國英格蘭戴文結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.Amesbury, Wiltshire, England: 英国維爾爾阿姆斯波利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hugh Le Moyne于大约1148年出生在英国維爾爾阿姆斯波利时，他的父亲Adam Le Moyne32岁，母亲Baroness NN Le Moyne Hamelyn28岁。他于1172年与Estcotte在英国英格兰戴文结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Moyne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 Norman, English: nickname from Old French moine, Anglo-Norman French muine, moingne ‘monk’. Some of the following early bearers without the definite article could alternatively belong with (2).
2  English:  see  Moon   (1).

variants.name: Moin
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Moyne, a nickname from Old French moine, Anglo-Norman French muine, moingne ‘monk’.
Muslim (mainly the Indian subcontinent): unexplained.

variants.name: Moon
meanings: Irish: variant of   Mohan  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Moyon in La Manche. Compare   Mounce  .
Americanized form of German   Mohn   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Moynan
meanings: Irish: perhaps a diminutive of   Moyne   or a reduced form of   Moynihan  .

variants.name: Moine
meanings: French: from moine ‘monk’, probably applied as an occupational name for a servant at a monastery or an ironic nickname for someone of monkish behavior or appearance. Compare Lemoine .

variants.name: Moynes
meanings: English: variant of Moyne with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh Le Moyne was born about 1148, in Amesbury, Wiltshire, England, his father, Adam Le Moyne, was 32 and his mother, Baroness NN Le Moyne Hamelyn, was 28. He married Estcotte in 1172, in Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugh Le Moyne est né environ 1148, à Amesbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, Adam Le Moyne, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Baroness NN Le Moyne Hamelyn, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Estcotte en 1172, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hugh Le Moyne um 1148 in Amesbury, Wiltshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Adam Le Moyne 32 und seine Mutter Baroness NN Le Moyne Hamelyn 28. Er hat 1172 in Devon, England, United Kingdom Estcotte geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh Le Moyne è nato il circa 1148 nel Amesbury, Wiltshire, England, il suo padre, Adam Le Moyne, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Baroness NN Le Moyne Hamelyn, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Estcotte nel 1172, nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Amesbury, Wiltshire, England: イギリスウィルトシュアイアエイメスブリー
ja.Devonshire, England, United Kingdom: イギリスイギリスデボン
ja.lifeSummary: Hugh Le Moyneが約1148年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアエイメスブリーで生まれた時，父Adam Le Moyneは32歳，母Baroness NN Le Moyne Hamelynは28歳であった。1172年にイギリスイギリスデボンでEstcotteと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh Le Moyne(이)가 대략 1148년 영국 윌츠히르 아메스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Adam Le Moyne(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Baroness NN Le Moyne Hamelyn(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1172년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 Estcotte(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh Le Moyne nasceu aproximadamente 1148, em Amesbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Adam Le Moyne, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Baroness NN Le Moyne Hamelyn, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Estcotte em 1172, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugh Le Moyne родился около 1148 г. в местности амесбури, уилцхир, Англия, его отцу по имени Adam Le Moyne было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Baroness NN Le Moyne Hamelyn, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Estcotte в 1172 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hugh Le Moyne nació aproximadamente 1148, en Amesbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Adam Le Moyne, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Baroness NN Le Moyne Hamelyn, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Estcotte en 1172, en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
